title: Candollea despecta
intro: Candollea despecta, also known as Candollea, is a shrub-like plant species native to South Africa, characterized by its small, white flowers and woody stems. Traditionally, Candollea despecta has been used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its potential anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. The bioactive constituents of Candollea despecta include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Candollea despecta is commonly prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture to leverage its health benefits and is also used in traditional African folk medicine.
taxonomy: Candollea despecta is a species of flowering plant in the family Santalaceae. The genus Candollea is a part of the order Santalales and is a group of parasitic plants.
